title: hamiltonia lanceolata
intro: Hamiltonia lanceolata, also known as 'Hangee' or 'Makomako', is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Elaeocarpaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and berries are rich in flavonoids and have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, Hamiltonia lanceolata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of the aforementioned conditions and promote overall well-being.
